title: Signal Integration
summary: Signal integration via signal-cli daemon in HTTP mode, covering daemon setup, SSE event streaming, JSON-RPC API, native bodyRanges text formatting, Note-to-Self pairing, E.164 phone number allowlisting, and health monitoring.
key_concepts: E.164 phone number format for contacts
Note-to-Self mode: message your own number

env_vars.name: SIGNAL_PHONE_NUMBER
env_vars.description: Your registered Signal phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567)

env_vars.name: SIGNAL_CLI_URL
env_vars.description: URL of the signal-cli HTTP daemon

env_vars.name: SIGNAL_ALLOWED_CONTACTS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated E.164 phone numbers allowed to interact

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Signal listener via SSE
Start signal-cli in HTTP daemon mode (run separately)

config_keys.description: Your Signal phone number in E.164 format
Allowed phone numbers in E.164 format
Use native Signal bodyRanges for text formatting (default: true)

code_examples.title: Start signal-cli daemon
code_examples.code:
# Register/link your number first (one-time)
signal-cli -u +15551234567 register
signal-cli -u +15551234567 verify CODE

# Start daemon
signal-cli -u +15551234567 daemon --http --port 8080

code_examples.title: Note-to-Self mode
code_examples.code:
signal:
  phone_number: "+15551234567"
  cli_url: "http://localhost:8080"
  note_to_self: true  # message yourself to interact with Hermes

gotchas: signal-cli must be installed and running as a separate daemon before starting the hermes gateway
Signal registration links the daemon to your phone number; you cannot use Signal on your phone simultaneously on the same number without linking as a secondary device
SSE connection to signal-cli may drop; hermes auto-reconnects but brief message gaps can occur
Groups are disabled by default; enable with groups: true and use @mention to trigger responses
Attachments (images, audio) are forwarded as file paths; vision pipeline processes images if the model supports it
bodyRanges formatting is Signal-native and not rendered in other clients; fall back to plain text if sharing sessions cross-platform
full_text:
## Signal

Connect Hermes to Signal via [signal-cli](https://github.com/AsamK/signal-cli) running in HTTP daemon mode.

### Prerequisites

Install signal-cli and register your phone number:

```bash
# One-time registration
signal-cli -u +15551234567 register
signal-cli -u +15551234567 verify CODE

# Start HTTP daemon
signal-cli -u +15551234567 daemon --http --port 8080
```

Verify the daemon is healthy:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health
# {"status": "ok"}
```

### Hermes config

```yaml
signal:
  phone_number: "+15551234567"
  cli_url: "http://localhost:8080"
  allowed_contacts:
    - "+447700900123"
```

Phone numbers must be in E.164 format ( followed by country code and number, no spaces or dashes).

### How it works

Hermes connects to signal-cli via SSE at  to stream incoming messages. Outgoing messages are sent via JSON-RPC POST to . No polling — event-driven.

### Note-to-Self mode

Interact with Hermes by messaging your own Signal number:

```yaml
signal:
  note_to_self: true
```

### Group chats

Enable group responses:

```yaml
signal:
  groups: true
```

In groups, Hermes responds only when mentioned.

### Native formatting (bodyRanges)

Signal supports native text formatting via bodyRanges (bold, italic, strikethrough, spoiler, monospace). Hermes uses these natively when  (default), which renders correctly in Signal clients.

### Attachments

Images are forwarded to the vision pipeline if the active model supports vision. Audio attachments are transcribed via the configured STT provider.

### Allowlist

```yaml
signal:
  allowed_contacts:
    - "+15551234567"
    - "+447700900123"
```

Leave empty to allow all contacts.
